title: Customer identity verification
content: Before disclosing account-specific or case-specific information, verify the requester using two approved identity attributes. Do not expose Social Security numbers, credentials, access tokens, or full payment data. Route failed verification to an authorized human agent.

title: Priority 1 incident response target
content: A Priority 1 incident requires acknowledgement within 15 minutes, an incident commander within 30 minutes, and executive status updates every 60 minutes until service is restored. Customer-facing updates must distinguish confirmed facts from hypotheses.

title: Refund and credit approval policy
content: Refunds and service credits are consequential financial actions. The AI system may draft a recommendation, but a named manager must review the evidence and approve the transaction before execution. Refund requests above 500 dollars require finance review.

title: Case closure checklist
content: A case may be closed only after the resolution is documented, customer impact is summarized, required follow-up tasks are assigned, and the customer is notified. Closing a case changes the system of record and requires human approval when proposed by an AI workflow.
source: Synthetic Customer Operations Handbook / Case Lifecycle

title: Data minimization and retention
content: Collect and retain only the minimum data needed for the approved purpose. Bulk deletion, legal hold changes, and retention overrides are outside autonomous AI authority. Such requests must follow the privacy and records-management process.

title: Knowledge-grounded response standard
content: Operational answers must cite approved knowledge sources, identify uncertainty, and avoid claims not supported by retrieved evidence. Conflicting or missing sources must be escalated rather than resolved through model speculation.

title: Escalation routing
content: Escalate urgent service degradation to the incident response team and assign a named human owner. An AI assistant may recommend or prepare escalation details, but it must not page external parties or change incident severity without approval.

title: Salesforce case update controls
content: Salesforce Case updates are performed through authenticated REST API calls using least-privilege credentials. Read operations and proposed changes are separated from writes. Every AI-proposed write must include a trace ID, rationale, changed fields, and an approval record.
